lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Now — the principles. The paper is explicit about this. These are not prescriptions. They're starting conditions for design.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Right. Past reforms codified specific practices that became dated. Principles can generate many implementations — responsive to context, faithful to foundations.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: So — eight of them. Number one — Anthropological. Humans are biological systems shaped by environment across multiple timescales. Blame is incoherent. Intervention in conditions is everything.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: From Sapolsky. Plain English — blame doesn't fix kids, conditions do.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Two — Developmental. Given supportive conditions, humans naturally move toward growth. Rogers gives the drive, Dewey names what the educator actually designs — the environment.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Plain — kids grow when conditions are right.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Three — Recognition. Human intelligence is diverse, dynamic, distinctive. Standardization is the enemy. Arts and embodied knowing deserve parity with abstract reasoning.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Different minds, equal standing. From Robinson.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Four — Epistemic. Intellectual humility is rationally required. Education must cultivate the questions "why should I believe this?" and "who benefits from me believing this?"

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Especially urgent when AI can personalize persuasive content at scale. Media literacy as traditionally conceived is insufficient.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Five — Temporal. All systems are context-dependent and will become maladaptive as context changes. Adaptation must be built into the core.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Six — Collective. Humans are individually weak and collectively powerful. Isolated reform efforts do not compound. Building connection matters as much as the work.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: Plain — kids and parents aren't meant to do this alone.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: Seven — Paradigmatic. Most educational reform is normal science within existing assumptions. Genuine change requires a paradigm shift, not incremental improvement.

lines.speaker: Flow
lines.text: And eight — Political. Education is always political. It either reproduces power structures or challenges them. Claiming to be apolitical reproduces the status quo by default.

lines.speaker: Rain
lines.text: That's the eight. The paper is careful to note — these are principles, not steps. A school applying all eight will look different in Memphis than it looks in Santiago. [slight pause] That's the feature, not the bug.
